user_request:
I want to create a table tracking AI companies that are actively hiring.

Columns needed:
- Company Name
- Website
- Is hiring for AI roles? (yes/no)
- Team size (approximate)
- Recent funding (last round)

Find about 10 companies across different AI sectors like research, healthcare, and enterprise.
Make sure to include both established companies and startups.

columns.name: Table Name
columns.description: The concise, descriptive name for the research table being defined

columns.name: Research Objective
columns.description: Clear summary of what entities this table tracks and the overall research goals
columns.validation_strategy: Verify that the objective clearly defines the entity type (companies, people, papers, etc.) and the research purpose

columns.name: Entity Type
columns.description: The category of real-world entities this table will track (e.g., companies, researchers, publications, products)
columns.validation_strategy: Confirm entity type is concrete and findable through web research, not abstract or meta-level

columns.name: ID Columns
columns.description: List of columns used to uniquely identify each entity row
columns.validation_strategy: Verify that ID columns are findable through public sources and uniquely identify entities without ambiguity

columns.name: Research Columns
columns.description: List of data columns that will be researched and validated for each entity
columns.validation_strategy: Ensure each research column has a specific validation strategy describing how to find and verify the data

columns.name: Number of Subdomains
columns.description: Count of distinct search subdomains for parallel row discovery
columns.validation_strategy: Verify subdomain count is between 2-5 and appropriate for the search complexity

columns.name: Total Target Rows
columns.description: Total number of candidate entities to discover across all subdomains
columns.validation_strategy: Confirm target rows are distributed across subdomains and account for deduplication multiplier

columns.name: Data Sources
columns.description: Primary and secondary sources for finding and validating entity data
columns.validation_strategy: Verify sources are reliable, publicly accessible, and appropriate for the entity type

columns.name: Search Query Quality
columns.description: Assessment of whether search queries are designed to yield multiple entities per query
columns.validation_strategy: Check that queries are list-based, aggregator-focused, or comparative rather than single-entity queries

search_strategy.description: This search strategy is for defining research table specifications themselves. We are looking for guidance on how to structure research tables, define columns for real entities, and create search strategies for finding those entities. The focus is on meta-level documentation of table design patterns.

subdomains.name: Table Definition Framework
subdomains.focus: Understanding the core components of a well-designed research table including entity type definition, ID column selection, and research column specification
search_queries: how to define research table structure and columns
best practices for entity identification in research databases
designing research tables for web-based entity discovery

subdomains.name: Search Strategy Design
subdomains.focus: Techniques for creating effective search strategies including subdomain definition, query formulation, and parallel discovery approaches
search_queries: search strategy design for entity discovery
parallel search techniques for finding multiple entities
query formulation for comprehensive entity coverage

subdomains.name: Data Validation Approaches
subdomains.focus: Methods for validating research data including source reliability assessment, recency evaluation, and relevancy scoring
search_queries: data validation strategies for research tables
source reliability assessment for web research
scoring and ranking entities for research quality

id_values.Table Name: Design of tables for the presentation and communication of data in ecology and evolutionary biology

final_rows.match_rationale: Directly addresses meta-level documentation of table design patterns, including column definition, alignment, and readability, but focuses on academic publishing rather than entity discovery or parallel search.

Directly addresses research table structure, column definition, and best practices for clarity and consistency, but lacks explicit guidance on entity ID column selection and search strategy documentation.
Comprehensive guide covering validation rules, error handling, and documentation—excellent for table specification, but less focused on source reliability assessment and recency scoring specifically.

id_values.Table Name: Data Validation: Key Techniques and Best Practices

final_rows.match_rationale: Details validation techniques and best practices, useful for defining table columns and validation logic, but lacks explicit guidance on source reliability and recency scoring.

id_values.Table Name: Enterprise UX: essential resources to design complex data tables

final_rows.match_rationale: Aggregates best practices and design patterns for complex data tables, including column customization and user interaction, but does not specifically address entity discovery or parallel search strategies.

id_values.Table Name: Designing effective data table UI – best practices and tips

final_rows.match_rationale: Covers UI best practices for data tables, including column alignment, filtering, and search, but lacks explicit guidance on entity discovery or parallel search techniques.
Provides clear examples and tips for structuring research tables, referencing, and captioning, but does not specifically cover entity ID columns or search strategies.

id_values.Table Name: How to Improve Data Quality Through Validation and Quality Checks

final_rows.match_rationale: Covers validation and quality checks broadly, with some attention to source reliability, but less specific on table design patterns and recency scoring.

id_values.Table Name: Pencil & Paper Data Table Design UX Patterns

final_rows.match_rationale: Focuses on UX best practices for data tables, including column alignment and readability, but is oriented toward software interfaces rather than research table specification or entity discovery.
Offers general formatting advice for tables, such as clear titles and headers, but lacks specific guidance on entity identification or research column specification.
